title: Literature review result (ranked recipe table)
description: The structured contract a research subagent returns to the orchestrator (literature=on only). Each finding is one actionable recipe extracted from the literature, graded for evidence and applicability, with a keep/reject verdict. The orchestrator ranks these, applies the evidence gate, and turns the keepers into the next hypothesis + verification todos.

iteration.description: The loop iteration this review was produced for.

research_scale.description: Depth tier used for this review.

questions_asked.description: The Level-1 and Level-2 questions this review set out to answer.

findings.description: Ranked best-first. Empty array is valid (means: no compelling evidence found).

summary.description: One-paragraph synthesis: the highest-value levers and what to try next.

question.description: The specific question this finding answers.

abstraction_level.description: 1 = high-level (architecture fit, prior approaches, does the literature show success). 2 = specific (init scheme, weight-decay dynamics, attention/cache choice, etc.).

claim.description: The concrete claim the literature supports, in one or two sentences.

recipe.description: The actionable configuration to implement, with exact values where the source gives them. Omit fields the source does not specify.

other_hparams.description: Any other exact settings reported (warmup steps, dropout, etc.).

strength.description: strong = multiple papers agree and/or a clean ablation isolates the gain; moderate = one solid source with results; weak = suggestive, no ablation.

has_ablation.description: Does a cited source ablate this change in isolation?

reported_gain.description: The improvement the source reports (e.g. '+2.1 top-1 on ImageNet').

citations.description: Sources backing the claim. At least one required — a finding with no citation is not grounded.

applicability_to_our_setup.description: Why this transfers to THIS data/model/budget — including cross-domain reasoning if the source is from another field. Note any mismatch (scale, data size, modality).

proposed_change.description: The concrete edit to make next iteration, phrased so the orchestrator can act on it directly.

files_to_edit.description: Which editable files the change would touch (must be within the loop's editable set).

how_to_verify.description: The metric/log/analysis to add or watch next run to tell whether this change actually helped. Becomes a verification todo.

verdict.description: Whether this passes the evidence gate and should be implemented.

verdict_reason.description: Why keep or reject (evidence strength x applicability x implementability).

snippet.description: The exact passage/section supporting the claim, if read.
